original_abstract:
The rapid maturation of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the proliferation of internet-connected sensory devices have transformed the traditional educational environment into the "Smart Classroom." This paper provides a comprehensive review of the technological landscape of these modern learning spaces, with a specific focus on the integration of AI-driven emerging technologies. We categorize the current state of the art into three functional pillars: classroom management (including computer vision for attendance and automated behavioral surveillance), advanced teaching aids (specifically educational robotics, chatbots, and immersive VR/AR/MR environments), and automated performance assessment for both students and educators. 

Beyond cataloging these advancements, the study offers a critical evaluation of the impact of AI on pedagogy, highlighting how these tools facilitate personalized and adaptive learning experiences. Through a detailed SWOT analysis, we identify the internal strengths and weaknesses of adopting AI in the classroom, alongside the external opportunities and threats—such as data privacy concerns, algorithmic bias, and the necessity for educator re-skilling. The paper concludes by outlining future research directions, emphasizing the need for low-cost, interoperable infrastructure and ethical data-handling frameworks to ensure that the next generation of smart learning environments is both effective and equitable. This survey serves as a strategic roadmap for both educators seeking to understand technological potential and AI professionals aiming to address the unique constraints of the educational sector.
humanized_abstract:
The rapid development of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and the abundance of the Internet of Things (IoT) sensing devices have turned the conventional educational environment into a so-called “Smart Classroom”. This paper provides a thorough survey of the Smart Classroom environment based on the application of new advanced technologies such as AI and its related emerging technologies. We classify the current state of the art in Smart Classroom into three main layers, namely: (i) Classroom management layer, which comprises computer vision-based attendance system, and behaviour detection system; (ii) Teaching aids layer, which involves educational robots, chatbots, and Virtual Reality (VR), Augmented Reality (AR), Mixed Reality (MR); and (iii) the automation of the assessment of learners’ and teachers’ performances.

Recent achievements in the development of educational systems, software applications, platforms and technologies are reported. In addition, the study examines the impact of AI on the educational process, and how it can be used to develop more intelligent learning systems that adapt the learning experience to individual needs. A SWOT analysis of the implementation of AI in education is given. Strengths, weaknesses, opportunities and threats of the use of AI in education are examined. Threats refer to negative forces or events that may prevent the adoption of the use of AI in education and which should be countered; opportunities refer to positive events or situations that could support the implementation of AI and its extension. These negative and positive forces are largely external to the technology itself. The paper concludes with a description of possible areas of future research, which include low cost infrastructure, interoperability, and the provision of infrastructure that handles data ethically. This survey can serve as a strategic guide for teachers seeking to leverage technological innovations as well as for AI researchers seeking to address the distinctive challenges of the educational domain.
